summary: This page distinguishes continuous-time versus discrete-time signals from analog versus digital signals, emphasizing that time indexing and amplitude quantization are different properties. It uses Fig. 1.5 to illustrate analog/digital and continuous-time/discrete-time combinations, introduces M-ary and binary digital signals, mentions analog-to-digital conversion by quantization, and begins the definition of periodic signals with the condition f(t) = f(t + T0).

figures.caption: Examples of Signals: (a) analog, continuous-time (b) digital, continuous-time (c) analog, discrete-time (d) digital, discrete-time.
